compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Why
Once rebar owns the OIDC provider and the domain-wide SSO stack (story ample-famous-cockatiel), rebar's infra docs must describe the ownership, dependencies, and operations (secret rotation, incident revoke) so they live in rebar's tree, not snap's dead repo.

## What
- Update `docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md`: add a subsection noting rebar now OWNS the GitHub OIDC provider (previously assumed "already exists in the account"), the `*.solutions.navateam.com` wildcard cert, and the `auth_host` domain-wide SSO stack — pointing at the ADR and the SSO runbook.
- Fix the stale comment in `infra/terraform/iam.tf` that said the OIDC provider "already exists in the account" now that rebar creates/owns it (already applied in the terraform story's commit).
- Add an ADR under `docs/adr/` recording the snap→rebar re-homing decision: adopt-vs-teardown, import-not-recreate, name-preservation tradeoff, cookie-secret idiom change, and the state migration.
- Add an SSO operations runbook under `infra/runbooks/sso-auth-host.md` adapted from snap's `auth/RUNBOOK.md`, covering: the auth-host architecture, the `/auth-solutions/*` secrets, cookie-signing-key rotation (SSM overwrite + Lambda `-replace`), Google-`client_secret` rotation, and the incident revoke lever. It also notes how a future rebar subdomain would opt into the SSO, but that section is descriptive prose — it does not gate this ticket.

## Scope
Documentation only (the terraform + module code land in story ample-famous-cockatiel). Four artifacts: the gerrit-aws-setup.md subsection, the iam.tf comment, the ADR, and the SSO runbook.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md` documents rebar's ownership of the OIDC provider + the wildcard cert + the auth_host SSO stack, linking the ADR + runbook.
- [ ] `infra/terraform/iam.tf`'s OIDC comment no longer claims the provider is externally owned.
- [ ] An ADR under `docs/adr/` records the re-homing decision and its tradeoffs (adopt-vs-teardown, import-not-recreate, name preservation, state migration).
- [ ] An `infra/runbooks/sso-auth-host.md` runbook covers the auth-host architecture, the `/auth-solutions/*` secrets, cookie-key + Google-secret rotation, and the incident revoke lever.
compiled_state.title: Document rebar ownership of the OIDC provider + auth_host SSO stack (docs + ADR + runbook)
